title: Ensure packet redirect sending is disabled

prose.en: Ensure packet redirect sending is disabled

description.en: ICMP Redirects are used to send routing information to other hosts. As a host itself does not act as a router (in a host only configuration), there is no need to send redirects.

rationale.en: An attacker could use a compromised host to send invalid ICMP redirects to other router devices in an attempt to corrupt routing and have users access a system set up by the attacker as opposed to a valid system.

audit.en:
Set the following parameters in `/etc/sysctl.conf` or a file in `/etc/sysctl.d/` ending in `.conf`:  

- `net.ipv4.conf.all.send_redirects = 0`
- `net.ipv4.conf.default.send_redirects = 0`

*Example:*  

Run the following script to set the active kernel parameters:  

**Note:** If these settings appear in a canonically later file, or later in the same file, these settings will be overwritten  

Impact:  

IP forwarding is required on systems configured to act as a router. If these parameters are disabled, the system will not be able to perform as a router.

```
#!/usr/bin/env bash

{
   sysctl -w net.ipv4.conf.all.send_redirects=0
   sysctl -w net.ipv4.conf.default.send_redirects=0
   sysctl -w net.ipv4.route.flush=1
}
```

```
# printf '%s\n' "net.ipv4.conf.all.send_redirects = 0" "net.ipv4.conf.default.send_redirects = 0" >> /etc/sysctl.d/60-netipv4_sysctl.conf
```

remediation.en:
Set the following parameters in `/etc/sysctl.conf` or a file in `/etc/sysctl.d/` ending in `.conf`:  

- `net.ipv4.conf.all.send_redirects = 0`
- `net.ipv4.conf.default.send_redirects = 0`

*Example:*  

Run the following script to set the active kernel parameters:  

**Note:** If these settings appear in a canonically later file, or later in the same file, these settings will be overwritten  

Impact:  

IP forwarding is required on systems configured to act as a router. If these parameters are disabled, the system will not be able to perform as a router.

```
#!/usr/bin/env bash

{
   sysctl -w net.ipv4.conf.all.send_redirects=0
   sysctl -w net.ipv4.conf.default.send_redirects=0
   sysctl -w net.ipv4.route.flush=1
}
```

```
# printf '%s\n' "net.ipv4.conf.all.send_redirects = 0" "net.ipv4.conf.default.send_redirects = 0" >> /etc/sysctl.d/60-netipv4_sysctl.conf
```

references: NIST SP 800-53 Rev. 5: CM-1, CM-2, CM-6, CM-7, IA-5
